opener.dateline:
United States Legation,
Bogotá, July 15. (Received July 27, 1903.)

div.p: Confidential. The situation is a little more favorable for the treaty. It is generally believed that it will be ratified, but with amendments. It is possible it can be passed without amendments; bat as the belief prevails that additional concessions can be secured, I consider it important that this Government be informed through the Colombian minister or me of your position as to the matter. In any event, I would appreciate secret instructions as a guide in case of emergency.
Yesterday the treaty was submitted to a special committee of nine in the Senate, four, and probably more, of whom are for ratification.
